context: Wednesday June 3 pre-market. Risk-off is accelerating, not stabilizing. Fear & Greed collapsed to 11 (EXTREME FEAR) from 23 yesterday — a sharp 12-point one-day drop that signals deepening capitulation. BTC broke down hard: -11.5% on the week with an intraday flush to $65,708, now ~$67,000. The selling is structural — an 11-session spot-ETF outflow streak (~$3.45B, the largest monthly exodus of 2026, led by IBIT/FBTC), MicroStrategy's first disclosed BTC sale in ~4 years, a Mt. Gox wallet transfer, and ~$1.8B of leveraged longs liquidated in 24h (largest since February). Brent crude is up a 3rd straight day on stalled US-Iran ceasefire talks, keeping inflation expectations elevated and the Fed boxed. Live tape: BTC $67,000 (-4.15%), XRP $1.24 (-2.23%, the relative-strength tell — only -7.2% wk vs majors), SUI $0.832 (-2.01%, worst at -17% wk after its THIRD 2026 outage), HBAR $0.088 (-2.68% 24h but +2.58% wk — the only name green on the week, carried by Canary ETF flows). Macro week is front-loaded: ADP + ISM Services today, Jobless Claims Thu, and NFP Friday Jun 5 (85K forecast, DOWN from 115K) is the week's BINARY event. FOMC Jun 16-17 = market-implied HOLD (3.50-3.75%). Trade plan: $65K is THE BTC anchor — below it opens $60K and drags the whole basket; a defended $65K sets up a relief-bounce flush. ZERO momentum longs into a deteriorating tape ahead of NFP. Sell rips in SUI; range-fade XRP/HBAR; respect that the macro flush overrides every idiosyncratic regulatory tailwind this week.

tickers.thesis: XRP is the relative-strength tell in a broad-risk-off tape: down only -7.2% on the week while BTC bled -11.5% and SUI -17%. The structural bid is the CLARITY Act — passed Senate Banking 15-9 on May 14, with a full Senate floor vote expected during June. Spot-ETF flows diverge hard from BTC's exodus: 7 XRP spot ETFs now hold ~$1.2B AUM, cumulative inflows ~$1.44B, and May was the strongest single inflow month of 2026 (+$84M net). That divergence is why XRP is holding the $1.20-$1.27 range while majors leak. Bias NEUTRAL — pinned mid-range, no momentum into a deteriorating macro week (NFP Fri). Tactical plan: range-fade $1.20 reclaim longs / $1.27 rejection shorts only; do NOT chase. A CLARITY floor-vote headline is the binary upside trigger; a BTC flush through $65K drags XRP to the $1.16 sweep first.

tickers.thesis: SUI is the worst name in the basket again (-17% on the week) and the damage is structural, not just beta. The network suffered its THIRD major outage of 2026 — three mainnet halts in 48 hours (May 28-29) traced to a v1.72 upgrade bug (gas-charging edge case + a latent on-chain-randomness bug). No funds were lost, but it's the third reliability failure since the 2023 launch, and it landed right on the CME SUI futures debut — kneecapping a key institutional catalyst. Add the June 1 unlock (+23.78M SUI of fresh supply) into fragile sentiment. Bias SHORT: $0.795 is the line being tested; a daily close below opens $0.75 then $0.70. Relief bounces into $0.851-$0.90 are sell-the-rip zones until reliability + macro stabilize. Only a confirmed $0.90 reclaim flips the short thesis.
levels: Late-May support, lost to the outage cascade — now supply
Today's low — THE line being tested

tickers.thesis: HBAR is the only name green on the week (+2.58%) while the rest of the basket bleeds — a genuine relative-strength standout driven by its own institutional flow story. The Canary spot HBAR ETF (Nasdaq: HBR) has absorbed ~549M HBAR (~1.3% of circulating supply) with ~$93M cumulative net inflows, and the Governing Council just added Accenture and FedEx (joining Google, IBM, Dell). CLARITY's 'digital commodity' framing is a structural tailwind for enterprise-focused chains. Bias NEUTRAL — the outperformance is real but HBAR is still a high-beta alt in a Fear=11 tape; it will not be immune if BTC loses $65K. Range trade: $0.085-$0.0906 is the box. A decisive daily close above $0.11 opens real upside; until then fade the extremes and respect that a macro flush (NFP Fri) overrides the idiosyncratic ETF bid.

insights.title: Sentiment deepening: F&G 11 (Extreme Fear), Δ -12 vs yesterday
insights.body: Crypto Fear & Greed Index printed 11 (Extreme Fear) at 08:29Z — -12 vs yesterday's reading. F&G under 30 historically aligns with bounce-watch zones; under 20 with capitulation. Combined with the recent price action and live headlines (see LIVE FEED), the regime is risk-off until a sweep-and-reclaim candle prints. No new longs until F&G stops falling AND BTC reclaims its 24h high.

insights.title: Wednesday Capitulation Deepens — F&G Crashed to 11 (Extreme Fear)
insights.body: Crypto Fear & Greed collapsed 23→11 in a single day into deep EXTREME FEAR — a 12-point one-day drop that marks an accelerating flush, not a stabilizing one. The driver is structural BTC selling: an 11-session spot-ETF outflow streak (~$3.45B, the largest monthly exodus of 2026), MicroStrategy's first disclosed BTC sale in ~4 years, a Mt. Gox transfer, and ~$1.8B of leveraged longs liquidated in 24h. BTC flushed to $65,708 intraday. Trade plan: $65K is THE anchor — a daily close below opens $60K and drags the entire basket; a defended $65K is the setup for a relief bounce as overleveraged positioning gets washed out. NO new longs until BTC reclaims $70K with volume. This is a defensive, sell-the-rip tape into NFP Friday.

insights.title: XRP Is the Relative-Strength Tell — ETF Inflows Diverge From BTC's Exodus
insights.body: While BTC bled -11.5% and SUI -17% on the week, XRP is down only -7.2% and holding the $1.20-$1.27 range. The reason is a genuine flow divergence: 7 XRP spot ETFs now hold ~$1.2B AUM with cumulative inflows ~$1.44B, and May was the strongest single inflow month of 2026 (+$84M net) — exactly as BTC ETFs hemorrhaged $3.45B. The structural bid is CLARITY (passed Senate Banking 15-9 May 14; full floor vote expected in June). Tactical: range-fade only — reclaim-of-$1.20 longs / $1.27-rejection shorts. A CLARITY floor-vote headline is the binary upside trigger; a BTC close below $65K drags XRP to the $1.16 sweep first. Do not chase mid-range into a deteriorating macro week.

insights.body: SUI is the worst name in the basket (-17% wk) and it earned it: three mainnet halts in 48 hours (May 28-29) traced to a v1.72 upgrade bug (gas-charging edge case + a latent on-chain-randomness bug). No funds were lost, but it's the THIRD reliability failure since the 2023 launch — and it landed on the CME SUI futures debut, kneecapping a key institutional catalyst. Add the June 1 unlock (+23.78M SUI) into fragile sentiment. Bias SHORT: $0.795 is the line; below it opens $0.75 then $0.70. Relief bounces into $0.851-$0.90 are sell-the-rip until reliability and macro stabilize. Only a confirmed $0.90 daily-close reclaim flips the thesis.

The Canary spot HBAR ETF (Nasdaq: HBR) has absorbed ~549M HBAR (~1.3% of supply) with ~$93M cumulative net inflows, and the Governing Council just added Accenture and FedEx. CLARITY's 'digital commodity' framing is a structural tailwind for enterprise chains. BUT: HBAR is still a high-beta alt in a Fear=11 tape — it will not be immune if BTC loses $65K. Range trade the $0.085-$0.0906 box; a decisive close above $0.11 is the upside unlock. Respect that NFP Friday can override the ETF bid in one print.

insights.title: NFP Friday Is the Binary — Front-Loaded Macro Week Into a Fragile Tape
insights.body: The labour-market data is front-loaded and culminates Friday: ADP (118K f/c) + ISM Services today, Jobless Claims Thursday (214K f/c), and NFP Friday Jun 5 with an 85K forecast — DOWN sharply from 115K prior, alongside 4.3% unemployment and +0.3% average hourly earnings. A soft/cold NFP into Fear=11 risk-off accelerates the flush (high-beta alts hit hardest); a hot print revives the hawkish-Fed narrative and pressures rate-sensitive risk. Either way it's the week's decision point — FOMC Jun 16-17 is a market-implied HOLD (3.50-3.75%) with CPI at 3.8% YoY keeping the Fed restrictive. Flatten or reduce size into the 8:30 AM ET Friday print; do not carry fresh leveraged exposure through it.

hidden_signals.body: Fear & Greed at 11 (Extreme Fear), Δ -12 vs yesterday — sub-30 zones historically align with bounce setups but require a sweep-and-reclaim candle to confirm

BTC spot-ETF 11-session outflow streak (~$3.45B) is the largest monthly exodus of 2026, led by IBIT/FBTC — institutional distribution, not retail panic; the streak is the single biggest tell that this leg lower is structural rather than a liquidation-only flush

MicroStrategy disclosed its FIRST BTC sale in ~4 years — a psychological regime break; the market's marquee corporate accumulator turning seller (even partially) removes a long-standing structural bid and is a sentiment signal well beyond the dollar amount

Coinbase premium has flipped NEGATIVE — US institutional demand is soft; combined with the ETF outflows, the two largest US-institutional gauges are both pointing the same (bearish) way, which is what's letting the majors leak through prior support

Whale divergence: the 1,000+ BTC cohort is contracting at a 2022-bear-market pace, YET on-chain shows 61,568 BTC (~$4.2B) accumulated in 30 days and long-term-holder supply at a record 15.8M — old hands selling into conviction buyers; an unresolved tension to watch

Galaxy Research frames the ETF outflows as a structural 'directional recalibration' toward equities (S&P at all-time highs) — i.e. a rotation that may be cyclical-to-structural, not a quick dip-buy; if correct, relief bounces get sold rather than chased

Brent crude up a 3rd straight day on stalled US-Iran ceasefire talks keeps inflation expectations elevated and the Fed boxed — the macro reason a rate-cut pivot stays off the table into the Jun 16-17 FOMC despite a weakening labour tape

XRP ETF cumulative inflows ~$1.44B (May +$84M, strongest month of 2026) diverge sharply from BTC's outflow exodus — a relative-strength tell; the only basket name with a live institutional inflow story while the majors see net redemptions

SUI's three mainnet halts in 48h (v1.72 upgrade bug) are a STRUCTURAL reliability risk, not just a price event — third failure since 2023 launch, landing on the CME futures debut; reliability fear, not macro beta, is why SUI is the basket's worst performer this week
